metadata.description: Opinionated Claude Code plugins for professional coding — language-specific authoring skills and expert subagents.

plugins.name: rust-dev
plugins.description: Idiomatic Rust authoring: progressive-disclosure skill plus rust-expert authoring subagent and slash commands for review and refactor.

plugins.name: android-dev
plugins.description: Android development plugin: Gradle build management, UI/UX design with Material 3 and Figma, multi-device emulator orchestration via bundled MCP stack, mock server generation, automated screenshot capture across phone and tablet form factors, release signing, and end-to-end publishing flows for F-Droid and Google Play.

plugins.name: plugin-dev
plugins.description: Lean, security-aware authoring kit for Claude Code plugins: skills for plugin structure / skill / command / agent / hook / MCP authoring, cross-host subagent scaffolding (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode), leak-audit and best-practices-sync passes for existing skills; agents for validation, skill review, and agent generation; create-plugin command for guided scaffolding. Covers 2026 hook events and surfaces.

plugins.name: release-promo
plugins.description: Drafts release-announcement posts for the platforms a project actually belongs on. Skills for Reddit (subreddit-aware, including Matrix subs r/matrixprotocol and r/matrixdotorg, plus r/selfhosted, r/programming, language and topic subs), This Week in Matrix, Hacker News Show HN, Lobsters, and Fediverse. The /promote-release command surveys the repo, picks eligible channels, and drafts one post per channel. Never autoposts.

plugins.name: stingy-agents
plugins.description: Three scope-bounded subagents that let a skill or an Opus caller offload bulk or mechanical work to a cheaper, faster model: readonly-scanner (Haiku) for bulk file I/O and grep, skill-rewriter (Sonnet) for spec-driven edits to existing files, and code-generator (Sonnet) for spec-driven file creation. Spend Opus tokens on judgment calls, Haiku and Sonnet tokens on everything else.

plugins.name: infra-build
plugins.description: Make a project buildable by the three publishing pipelines under ~/dev/infra: utils (Debian .deb via pkgskel + reprepro), build-for-mac (Rust → macOS .pkg via GitHub Actions), and publish-images (multi-arch Docker → DockerHub/GHCR/Quay/GitLab/ECR). Skills audit readiness, scaffold the missing file layout, and register the project in both the data file and the workflow YAML in lockstep so the two never drift.

plugins.name: git-github
plugins.description: Everyday git/GitHub operations for Claude Code: draft commits and PRs that match the repo's style, run a single-tool authoritative code review, dispatch the same diff to sibling CLIs (codex/gemini/opencode) for multi-model second opinions, audit code comments for explanatory value, audit GitHub Actions workflows for errors and outdated actions, review and tighten READMEs, audit dependency licenses and pick a compatible project LICENSE, and cut release tags with optional GitHub releases. Skills auto-trigger on natural language; nothing autoposts, force-pushes, or rewrites history. Hands off announcement drafting to release-promo.

plugins.name: planning
plugins.description: Four-skill pipeline that turns a vague idea into executed work: brainstorming validates the design via question-driven exploration, planning-projects produces a staged plan with phase gates and Red-Green cycles, executing-plans drives the plan with stage-gate checks, and dispatching-parallel-agents fans out independent tasks marked Parallel YES. Skills hand off to each other and were designed as a unit.
